"Look, the trees
are turning
their own bodies
into pillars

of light,
are giving off the rich
fragrance of cinnamon
and fulfillment,

the long tapers
of cattails
are bursting and floating away over
the blue shoulders

of the ponds,
and every pond,
no matter what its
name is, is

nameless now.
Every year
everything
I have ever learned

in my lifetime
leads back to this: the fires
and the black river of loss
whose other side

is salvation,
whose meaning
none of us will ever know.
To live in this world

you must be able
to do three things:
to love what is mortal;
to hold it

against your bones knowing
your own life depends on it;
and, when the time comes to let it
go,
to let it go."

-Mary Oliver

You always feel one step out of place.
If your birth date falls on 4, 13, 22, or 31, this might sound familiar. 🤔

Ruled by Rahu, you’re not wired like most people around you. Even when you’re doing everything right, life often feels more complicated than it should.

7 uncomfortable truths about Mulank 4:

• You’ve felt misunderstood for most of your life
• Success comes late, but expectations come early
• Your life can change dramatically in a matter of days
• You question everything, including yourself
• You often carry stress without telling anyone
• You don’t fit neatly into traditional paths
• The biggest lessons in your life come through struggle

The strange thing?😨

Many people think you’re unlucky because they only see the chaos.

What they don’t see is how many times you’ve started over.
How many times you’ve adapted.
How many times you’ve survived situations that would’ve broken someone else.

Your path isn’t meant to look normal.

It’s meant to change you. 🙏🏻

In spirituality, tears in a temple are often seen differently.
It is said that when you stand before the Divine, your soul recognizes a presence that the mind cannot fully understand.

The ego becomes quieter.
The heart becomes softer.
And emotions that have been hidden for months, sometimes years, begin to flow out.
These tears are not always a sign of pain.
They can be a sign of surrender.
Of gratitude.
Of karmic release.
Or simply the feeling of being deeply seen by the Divine.
Perhaps that’s why so many people enter a temple with a heavy heart…
And leave feeling lighter than before. 🙏❤️

Mulank 2 & 6 are often considered one of the strongest and most emotionally balanced pairs in numerology ❤️

Why?

The Space C-Bomb was my favorite high calorie snack on the Space Station.
Take one lemon curd cake packet, smash in a big handful of macadamia nuts, then drown that bad boy in vanilla pudding. What would be a sad, sliding mess on Earth turns into a bite-sized cloud of yum in space!

My heart is heavy today for the Edwards AFB and flight test family after yesterday’s tragic B-52 loss. These eight brave souls were pushing the bounds of possible and their families are in our prayers.

29 years ago I married my smoking-hot best friend @elizabethafischer the absolute center of my universe. Some missions are worth repeating, so every 10 years we suit up again and say “I do”. Next year will mark 30 years and our fourth wedding; some things you celebrate over and over because they’re just that good.

The Earth’s constant rotation means you’ve got to have some serious firepower to get a signal to the lunar orbit. But building a global network that powerful would be incredibly expensive. It turns out those same monster dishes used to study things like distant galaxies and black holes aren’t super useful when the Moon is out. The Moon’s thermal heat and light flood the night sky, making it more difficult to study; consequently, those giant radio dishes often have more open time. So Intuitive Machines rents those dishes during their lower demand periods because radio waves don’t care how bright the Moon is. Sharing expensive toys vs buying new ones = win/win

On June 2, 1966, NASA’s Surveyor 1 made history as the first American spacecraft to achieve a soft landing on the Moon. 58 years later, Odysseus became the first commercial spacecraft to land on the Moon. Surveyor 1 helped us learn landing was possible. Odysseus showed the world that today, anyone with bold engineering can achieve amazing things. What began as a superpower showdown has become a frontier open to dreamers, engineers, and visionaries.

8 years ago today I hung up the blue NASA flight suit for the last time. 
What a wild, incredible ride those 9 years were making a lifetime of memories with the best team on (and off) the planet. 
It was bittersweet then, but looking back it was the perfect doorway to the next chapter and I’m grateful to keep chasing the stars every day at Intuitive Machines..

The International Space Station isn’t just an engineering feat, it’s a floating embassy of human cooperation! I had the lucky task of organizing the flags for a picture prop of the Expedition 52 crew picture. Each flag displayed represents a nation’s investment, ingenuity, and dreams—a daily reminder that humanity’s greatest achievements don’t belong to one country. They belong to us all.

Nothing quite compares to the anticipation of a spacewalk, but there is REALLY nothing like finding out you’re going to do one on the fly. 9 years ago this week we had an external hardware failure on a Saturday. The next day a contingency (or emergency) spacewalk was approved, and by Tuesday, @astro_peggy and I were headed back outside. Less than 72 hours from failure to EVA is faster than a NASCAR race by space standards. And I would do it again in a second!

If you want to do something unprecedented, you can’t follow a map—you have to create the path. When you hit a roadblock, you don’t stop; you innovate around it, invent a new solution, and move forward in a way no one has before. 
At @intuitivemachines , every constraint challenges us to see problems differently. Rather than accepting limits, we treat them as invitations to reimagine what’s possible. Because the best solutions are rarely the obvious ones.

9 years ago today I performed my first spacewalk with the space ninja herself @astro_peggy . It’s a surreal experience. You come out of the hatch feet-down, and right there underneath you is planet Earth—just this massive, beautiful, living thing rushing by at 17,500 miles an hour. You can’t help but be mesmerized staring down at the biggest slice of awesome pie you’ve ever seen. But as crazy as that moment is you have to click it off, compartmentalize and go to work.

Did you know that the American flags on the Moon have been bleached by decades of exposure to the Moon’s harsh conditions? Apollo gave us the greatest adventure in human history and those flags were perfect for their moment. But this time we’re using current technology so when the next American flag goes on the Moon it’s gonna stay red, white, and blue for a long, long time!
This is a perfect example of how easily you can lose things on the Space Station. We had no idea the clip was gone until we went to grab it and it wasn’t there.

Orbital sunset and solar array, or light saber?
#Maythe4th #Maythe4thbewithyou

So dang happy for my dear friend @astro_peggy , aka. The undisputed GOAT 🐐, Space Ninja 🥷, Chuck Norris of space.  Last night @rnasafoundation gave her the National Space Trophy for lifetime achievement and continued badassery 😉. Congrats Peggy!!
Today is the day I launched into space and nine years later the thrill hasn’t faded one bit! Grateful for the ride of a lifetime and the incredible team that made it possible. 🚀

NASA technology reaches farther than you might think!

Many technologies developed for space exploration improve life on the ground after a company transforms it into a commercial product. This repurposed tech is called a spinoff.

From cell phone cameras to memory foam pillows, you likely use NASA spinoffs in your everyday life. Since the 1950s, space technology has contributed to many products available to people on Earth.

Swipe to see a few recent NASA spinoffs:

1. We’ve created an affordable, 3D-printable metal alloy that can withstand the extreme temperatures of spaceflight. Now, it is used in industries like aviation as a durable, affordable material.

2. Robotic assistant systems built to withstand space are being used to guide the design of underwater robots built for the deep sea.

3. We've shared what we learned about growing crops in space with researchers outside NASA to grow crops indoors more efficiently.

4. We sought to make plastics better insulators by infusing them with aerogel, but found that it also made the plastic more durable. Now, companies are finding uses for this plastic in everyday objects, like beverage lids and flowerpots. 

#NASA #Technology #Spinoff #TheMoreYouKnow

Long day? ☀️

Aboard the International Space Station are a handful of small lab facilities called gloveboxes.

These are enclosed spaces that allow the astronauts on the @ISS to complete experiments with materials that need to be contained, like bacteria or reactive chemicals. Microgravity can cause these potentially hazardous materials to float—that’s where the gloveboxes come in handy!

There are two primary gloveboxes in use in the orbiting lab—the Microgravity Science Glovebox (MSG) and Life Science Glovebox (LSG)—with other portable ones that can be set up when needed. Swipe to see the main gloveboxes in action:

1. The Life Science Glovebox is, as the name suggests, ideal for biology experiments. It is built in a way to ensure no biological material gets in or out—which is necessary when working with cells and living tissue.

2. The Microgravity Science Glovebox is a space meant to mimic working laboratory conditions on Earth. It keeps the crew safe when working on experiments that include biological substances, fluids, fine particles, and even fire. The MSG was installed in 2002 and, as of this month, has been running experiments for more than 100,000 hours!

#NASA #ISS #Astronaut

Cosmic sculptors in action ⭐

You’re looking at a fresh view of the young star cluster called NGC 346, home to over 2,500 newborn stars. This cluster is a center of star formation that lies 200,000 light-years away from Earth in the Small Magellanic Cloud, a satellite galaxy of our Milky Way. 

This photo by @NASAHubble combines infrared, optical, and ultraviolet data to showcase this ghostly celestial sight.

The pink gas and dark dust snaking around the cluster are the remnants of the stars’ birthplace. The massive stars within this cluster produce intense radiation and fierce stellar winds that carve and shape the nebula that surrounds them.

#NASA #Webb #Hubble #Stars

Reaching new heights 🛫

Our experimental X-59 aircraft, which is designed to reach supersonic speeds with a quiet sonic ‘thump’, reached a new altitude of 55,000 feet on Friday, June 12.

It also flew at 1.4 times the speed of sound — about 924 miles an hour. However, for these early supersonic flights, the X-59 has been accompanied by an F-15 research aircraft whose sonic booms obscure any noise the X-59 makes. Future flights will track the X-59’s shock wave and acoustic signature to ensure its quiet thump is intact.

Each flight brings us closer to the goal of our Quesst mission: helping future air travelers get where they need to go faster without disturbing those below. Follow @NASAAero for the latest updates on the X-59 and Quesst!

Credit: NASA

#NASA #Supersonic #Aviation

Meet Europa 🧊

It's the fourth-largest moon of Jupiter. Europa is about 90% the size of our own Moon, but if it suddenly appeared in Earth's orbit, it would shine five times as bright in the night sky. This is because of its reflective, icy crust, which hides a massive subterranean ocean that could hold life beyond our home world.

Our Europa Clipper mission is on its way to learn more about this mysterious moon, and should reach Jupiter in April 2030. This view of Europa was captured by our Juno spacecraft in 2022.

Abstract plant art 🎨⁣
 ⁣
Along the Vetrivier (Vet River) in South Africa, a colorful patchwork of circular and rectangular fields spreads across what is otherwise a semi-arid part of the Free State province. In this image, each color carries physical meaning, providing clues about crop types and revealing how they changed over the course of the Southern Hemisphere’s growing season.⁣
 ⁣
The NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar) satellite captured the data for this visualization between November 2025 and March 2026, using radar to "see" vegetation’s structure instead of its color. By combining this radar data, scientists built a compact summary of seasonal agricultural activity and change.⁣
⁣
#NASA #Farming #Plants #Science

This galaxy packs a punch

You’re looking at Messier 64, or the Black Eye Galaxy. This galaxy is located 17 million light-years away in the constellation Coma Berenices.

Messier 64 is a spiral galaxy like our own Milky Way, but it has a unique internal current that sets it apart. The gas in the outer regions of the galaxy rotates in the opposite direction of its internal regions. This contrasting motion might be caused by this galaxy merging with another one over a billion years ago. Where the opposing swirls of gases collide, new stars form.
 
This photo is a combination of views from @NASAHubble and @NASAWebb.
 
#NASA #Galaxy #Webb #Hubble

Introducing Artemis III.

Four astronauts. Three launches. Two dockings. One splashdown.

In 2027, the Artemis III mission will practice docking the Orion spacecraft with two lunar landers in low Earth orbit. On future Artemis missions, landers will bring astronauts to the lunar surface. While Artemis III will not land on the Moon, it will test the complex capabilities we need to return humanity there — this time to stay.

NASA astronauts @AstroKomrade, @Astro_FrankRubio, and @Astro_AndreD will join @EuropeanSpaceAgency astronaut @Astro_Luca on this historic mission.

Credit: NASA

An image that feels like summer ☀️

Beyond the Orion Nebula is a long and massive filament of cold gas and dust, divided into four parts and collectively called the Orion Molecular Clouds. This image, captured by @NASAWebb, shows just a small portion of one of the clouds.

Every stage of star formation — from the youngest stellar embryos, to protoplanetary discs, to newly-minted pre-main sequence stars — is contained within this scene (captured by Webb’s Near-Infared Camera) which stretches 150 light-years across. 

The intense star-forming activity has produced an impressive display of billowing outflows and sparkling stars atop swirling layers of gas and dark, obscuring clouds. Only in the infrared do we see protostars begin to shine out from the cocoons of dust in which they are born.

Read more at the link in @ESAWebb’s bio.

Tune into our Instagram Live on Tuesday, June 9, at 11:30 a.m. EDT (1530 UTC) to meet the astronauts flying aboard Artemis III, the mission that will test rendezvous and docking capabilities with commercial landers in low Earth orbit. Journey to the center of the galaxy cluster

This spiral galaxy, known as Messier 88 or M88, can be found 63 million light-years from Earth in the Virgo Cluster, a collection of more than 1,000 galaxies held together by gravity. 

As its galaxies swirl and mix, M88 will spend hundreds of millions of years slowly drifting toward the innermost reaches of the Virgo Cluster. Along the way, it will get pushed and pulled by other galaxies in the cluster — reshaping it forever on its cosmic journey.

#NASA #Galaxy #TravelAddict

You’re not unc. You’re not chopped. You’re a vibrant, sparkly cosmic squid 🦑

This dazzling galaxy goes by many names: NGC 1068, Messier 77, and the Squid Galaxy. It’s located about 45 million light-years away from Earth, and can be seen from the ground with a small telescope in the constellation Cetus, or the Whale.

Like our own Milky Way, the Squid Galaxy has a black hole at its center. However, its black hole is twice as massive as our galaxy's black hole and about 15 million times the mass of our Sun.

This image is a combination of views from multiple telescopes, including @NASAChandraXray, @NASAHubble, and @NASAWebb.

शादी में हो रही है देरी? गुरुवार को करें ये चमत्कारी उपाय! 💍✨🌿

अगर आपकी या आपके घर में किसी की शादी में लगातार रुकावटें आ रही हैं, बात बनते-बनते बिगड़ जाती है या उम्र निकलती जा रही है, तो इसका सीधा कारण कुंडली में बृहस्पति (Jupiter) का कमज़ोर होना हो सकता है। 📉🛑

गुरु ग्रह को अनुकूल करने और जल्दी विवाह के योग बनाने के लिए केले के पेड़ का यह दिव्य उपाय बेहद असरदार है:
कैसे करें यह 'शीघ्र विवाह' उपाय? 📜✍️
हर गुरुवार को दीपक: प्रत्येक बृहस्पतिवार को केले के पेड़ के नीचे शुद्ध घी का एक दीपक (Deepak) ज़रूर जलाएं।
महामंत्र का जाप: दीपक जलाते समय स्क्रीन पर दिए गए इस विशेष बृहस्पति मंत्र का सच्चे मन से जाप करें।
रोज़ाना जल अर्पित करें: विवाह की बाधाओं को जड़ से खत्म करने के लिए रोज़ाना केले के पेड़ में जल चढ़ाना शुरू करें।

⚠️ सबसे ज़रूरी नियम: याद रखें, यदि आप यह उपाय कर रहे हैं या गुरुवार का व्रत रखते हैं, तो बृहस्पतिवार के दिन भूलकर भी केले का सेवन (खाना) न करें। इस दिन केले के पेड़ की केवल पूजा की जाती है। 🍌❌

विश्वास रखिए: देवगुरु बृहस्पति और साक्षात श्री हरि नारायण आपकी पुकार ज़रूर सुनेंगे और आपके विवाह के योग बहुत जल्द बन जाएंगे!

भीम ने पूरी निष्ठा से यह व्रत किया और उन्हें मोक्ष की प्राप्ति हुई। इसी कारण इसे भीमसेनी एकादशी भी कहा जाता है।

पित्रों की मुक्ति और तरक्की: इस दिन की गई पूजा और व्रत का फल जब आप अपने पितरों को समर्पित करते हैं, तो उनका कष्ट दूर होता है और उनका सीधा आशीर्वाद आपके परिवार की तरक्की के रास्ते खोल देता है।🕊️
